what_it_does: Implements a per-turn context-variable resolver for the right-hand-man: a universal registry of variable types, a TurnContext substrate, and concrete resolvers that read the live graph and trial store to produce the context bundle a turn receives.
observations: TurnContext is a Pydantic BaseModel carrying graph, selection, recent events, an optional store, and an optional trial_session.
ContextVariable is a runtime_checkable Protocol requiring name, cost, and resolve(ctx).
REGISTRY maps variable names to variable type instances; register() validates structure and refuses silent overwrites.
resolve_context() resolves a list of requested names and raises on unknown names.
Concrete variables cover selection, run-state, rules, trajectory, recall-slice, and three trial_* variables.
Trial variables read the same store via trial://<session>/transcript and helpers _trial_transcript_addr / _resolve_trial_session.
Rules and RecallSlice are placeholders returning empty collections until S7 governance and a corpus exist.
A self-check under __main__ exercises selection, run_state, recall_slice, unknown-name failure, and trial variables with no store.
standouts: The design explicitly mirrors node-input resolution: context variables resolve from a substrate rather than being hard-coded in prompts.
A cost vocabulary (cheap, loads-model, loads-corpus) lets a turn assembler budget context resolution.
Trial recordings are first-class context variables, making a recorded session ground truth for later turns.
Fail-loud behavior is applied consistently: unknown names, missing selected nodes, and missing store expectations raise rather than fall back.
conventions: Uses pydantic BaseModel with ConfigDict(arbitrary_types_allowed=True) for the store field.
Uses runtime_checkable Protocol so register() can structurally validate variable types.
Register returns the variable and stores it in a module-level REGISTRY.
Placeholder resolves return empty collections with comments explaining the future wiring.
Self-check is included in __main__ with assertions and printed output.
concerns: TrialTranscript.resolve returns {} when a store is present but the session has no transcript ref, while the comment says fail-loud is left to Suite, creating a soft boundary.
_resolve_trial_session and trial resolvers assume store methods (list_sessions, load_session, head, get_content) that are not defined or documented in this file.
RunState buckets only running, failed, and idle/ready; terminal statuses are silently omitted from the idle bucket comment but not represented.
TrialFeedback filters on the literal kind string trial.feedback, which is not defined locally.
notes: This file is the seeing half of the RHM: it turns the live document and recorded trial store into a resolved, named context bundle each turn.
questions: What exactly is the store interface contract for list_sessions, load_session, head, and get_content?
How does the turn assembler use the cost labels to budget or order context resolution?
What is the expected format and content of ctx.recent and the trial transcript turns?
How are variable names requested per provider or per turn configuration?
What will the S7 governance registry look like when Rules is no longer a placeholder?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it is a resolution engine for context variables with a universal registry and built-in variables.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the assistant's prompt context a derived, per-turn artifact of the same runtime substrate that fires nodes, so adding a new context dimension only requires registering one variable type.
novelty: It treats recorded trial sessions as ordinary context variables resolved from a shared CAS store, letting post-trial debrief data flow back into subsequent turns through the same registry.
contribution: It contributes a typed, registry-based context-variable resolver plus a built-in library of graph-derived and trial-store-derived variables that any turn assembler can invoke uniformly without provider-specific hardcoding.
summary_for_embedding: A Pydantic and Protocol-based context-variable resolver for an RHM turn, with a universal registry, cost labels, and built-in variables that read the live graph and trial-recorded store.
intention_for_embedding: To let an assistant's per-turn context be resolved from the same runtime substrate as node inputs rather than baked into static prompts.

symbols.does: Pydantic model for the live substrate a single RHM turn resolves context against.
symbols.description: TurnContext holds the graph, selected node ids, recent event slice, optional resolver store, and optional trial_session. It uses arbitrary_types_allowed so the store field can carry a non-Pydantic resolver object.

symbols.does: Runtime-checkable Protocol defining a context-variable type.
symbols.description: ContextVariable requires a name string, a Cost label, and a resolve(ctx) method. It mirrors the resolver protocol pattern so variable types can be validated structurally before registration.

symbols.does: Reads the TurnContext substrate and returns the variable's value for this turn.
symbols.description: The protocol method signature that every concrete variable type must implement; it receives the full TurnContext and returns an arbitrary value.

symbols.does: Validates and inserts a ContextVariable into the universal REGISTRY.
symbols.description: register checks that the object satisfies the ContextVariable protocol and that no different variable already owns the same name, then stores it and returns it. Duplicate registration of the same instance is allowed.

symbols.does: Resolves a requested list of variable names against the current TurnContext.
symbols.description: resolve_context looks up each name in REGISTRY, calls resolve(ctx), and collects the results into a bundle. It raises KeyError for unknown names, enforcing no silent fallbacks.

symbols.does: Context variable that returns the node records currently selected on the canvas.
symbols.description: Selection resolves by mapping node ids in ctx.selection to NodeInstance records in ctx.graph. It raises KeyError if a selected id is missing from the graph.

symbols.does: Builds the list of selected NodeInstance records from the live graph.
symbols.description: The resolve method builds an id-to-node lookup and returns the matching records in selection order, failing loud on any inconsistency between selection and graph.

symbols.does: Context variable that buckets node ids by runtime status.
symbols.description: RunState scans ctx.graph.nodes and partitions ids into running, failed, and idle lists based on each node's status field, returning the buckets plus an implicit summary.

symbols.does: Returns running, failed, and idle node id buckets.
symbols.description: The resolve method iterates over the graph nodes, classifies statuses into running, failed, or idle/ready, and returns a dictionary of lists.

symbols.does: Context variable placeholder for governing rules from the future S7 registry.
symbols.description: Rules currently returns an empty list. The comment notes it will read the governance engine once S7 exists, keeping the returned shape a list for schema compatibility.

symbols.does: Returns an empty list as a placeholder for future governance rules.
symbols.description: Until the S7 governance registry is wired, this resolve returns [] so callers can depend on the list shape without receiving real rules.

symbols.does: Context variable that exposes the recent path or event log slice.
symbols.description: Trajectory simply returns ctx.recent, giving the turn access to what was recently done or decided.

symbols.does: Returns the recent event list from the TurnContext.
symbols.description: This is a cheap passthrough resolve that hands back the recent path stored on the context.

symbols.does: Context variable placeholder for a relevant slice of past work from a corpus.
symbols.description: RecallSlice returns [] until a corpus exists. Its cost is labeled loads-corpus to signal that real implementation will perform an expensive corpus read.

symbols.does: Returns an empty list because no corpus exists yet.
symbols.description: The placeholder resolve returns nothing yet, per C6 section 5, so the brain leans on explicit context until recall is implemented.

symbols.does: Helper that builds the trial://<session>/transcript CAS reference string.
symbols.description: This private function mirrors the address format used by Suite to ensure trial transcript reads and writes point at the same CAS-backed object.

symbols.does: Helper that picks the trial session to read from the context or the latest recorded one.
symbols.description: It returns ctx.trial_session if set, otherwise scans the store for the lexically latest session record whose kind equals trial. It returns None when no store or no trial session exists.

symbols.does: Context variable that returns the full recorded transcript of a trial session.
symbols.description: TrialTranscript resolves the target session, reads the CAS content at trial://<session>/transcript through the store, and returns the transcript dict, or {} when no session or store is available.

symbols.does: Loads and returns the full trial transcript dict from the store.
symbols.description: The resolve method uses _resolve_trial_session and _trial_transcript_addr to locate the transcript CAS object, then returns its content or an empty dict if none is found.

symbols.does: Context variable that returns which cast member a trial session was with.
symbols.description: TrialCharacter loads the session record through the store and returns its character field, or None when no session or store is available.

symbols.does: Returns the character field from the resolved trial session record.
symbols.description: The resolve method obtains the session id, loads the session record from the store, and returns the character value if present.

symbols.does: Context variable that returns Tim's spoken feedback turns from a trial transcript.
symbols.description: TrialFeedback resolves the transcript and filters its turns list to entries whose kind equals trial.feedback, returning the operator verdict-in-flight as a list.

symbols.does: Filters the trial transcript for feedback turns and returns them.
symbols.description: The resolve method loads the trial transcript and returns only the turns marked as trial.feedback, or an empty list when no transcript is available.
